TextENGNew: ...I chose to believe Rika-chan's words, and follow where she led.

Honestly, I could not bring myself to think this passive approach was right—and regardless of the circumstances, having to stand by in silence while Satoko was dragged into misery filled me with bitter shame.
...But having declared my faith in Rika-chan, if this was truly the best way to avoid a wretched future, then throwing myself into it completely was the one thing I could do now.
And so the prophecy—or perhaps I should call it a divine decree—proved true. Every event that followed unfolded precisely as Rika-chan had foretold.
changeReason: Changes 'divine premonition' to 'divine decree'; JPN 神がかり的な断言 means a god-like assertion/decree, not a premonition (which implies foresight rather than authoritative statement).

TextENGNew: Rina Mamiya's body was found in the village drainage ditch, a mutilated corpse.
changeReason: New adds 'a mutilated corpse,' reflecting JPN 惨殺体 (brutally murdered body) which the old translation omitted.

TextENGNew: In the immediate aftermath... Satoko was dragged back to the Houjou household by her uncle's hand.
changeReason: Changes 'returned and took her' to 'dragged back by her uncle's hand'; JPN 叔父の手によって戻された uses passive voice emphasizing Satoko was forced back against her will.

TextENGNew: When Rena and Shion learned the truth, they flew into a rage and clamored to rescue Satoko at once—but I threw myself bodily in their path, desperate to calm them, to find some legal means by which the three of us could act on Satoko's behalf—
...and so we all descended en masse upon the welfare consultation center in Okinomiya, crying out about Satoko's desperate plight.
changeReason: Changes 'child consultation center' to 'welfare consultation center'; JPN 生活相談所 means life/welfare consultation center, not a child-specific agency.

TextENGNew: As the class representative, and as Mion's close friend—somewhat to my own surprise—my name had spread throughout the entire village.

I was able to rally the village's support far faster than even Rika-chan had expected, and even managed to secure the consent of the most formidable obstacle: Oryou Sonozaki, head of the Sonozaki clan.
As a result, through the tireless effort of so many people, we succeeded in wresting Satoko from her uncle's hands just a few days later—!
And yet.
The Satoko we rescued... was no longer herself.
I did not understand why.
Even Rika-chan only shook her head, her face drained of color.
And Satoko would never again show us that bright, bubbling smile of hers—

...She simply lay in the clinic bed, staring up at the ceiling with hollow, lightless eyes, utterly still—no matter who spoke to her, no matter who reached out, just staring upward, a doll with nothing left inside.
...Layer upon layer of anguish beyond even Rika-chan's reckoning must have shattered something in her mind... In the end, had we simply been too late...?
...In a situation like that, revealing the truth to our friends would not have heartened them in the slightest.

As a last resort, Rika-chan—who had taken shelter with the Sonozaki family to escape Takano's reach—was captured by the Mountain Dog unit in exchange for Satoko, who had been abducted as leverage beforehand.
—What happened after that. ...I was killed moments later. I would never know.
Did Keiichi think me a cold woman?
changeReason: Changes statement 'Keiichi must think me' to question 'Did Keiichi think me'; JPN だろうか is a questioning/wondering form, expressing uncertainty rather than assertion.

TextENGNew: ...I truly did not want any of this. But from the moment I spotted Rina in the library, I had thought and thought and thought... and this was the only conclusion I could reach.
Satoko would be taken away by her uncle Teppei, subjected to his cruelty, and broken down bit by bit...
changeReason: New adds 'subjected to his cruelty' reflecting JPN ひどい仕打ちを受けて (being subjected to terrible treatment), which the old translation condensed to just 'mentally cornered.'

TextENGNew: I had always assumed that was the worst possible scenario. Whenever it played out, Keiichi—or perhaps Shion or Rena—would almost certainly turn murderous toward Teppei, and attempt to remove him by force.
changeReason: New explicitly says 'turn murderous,' capturing JPN 殺意を抱き (harbor murderous intent), while old used the vaguer 'resort to violence.'

TextENGNew: And then, as a consequence—

—consumed by guilt over the killing, they would grow suspicious of everyone around them, and destroy themselves from within. And with them in that state, I could never ask them for help, and in time I would be killed by Takano's hands...
I had always believed that chain could never be broken. So whenever the world set off in that direction, I would tell myself it was simply bad luck and give up before it had truly begun.
But.

In the previous world, Keiichi shattered that wretched scenario magnificently—through nothing but his own will and conviction. He rescued Satoko, and even swept away the prejudice that the villagers had held against her for so long.
And then I understood. The scenario I had always considered the worst—the one I had never once thought to use as a path forward—was in fact the method that brought me closest to the goal.
changeReason: New adds 'the one I had never once thought to use as a path forward,' reflecting JPN 今まで考えもしなかった (had never even considered), which the old translation omitted.

TextENGNew: For that... the only way was to... *abandon* Satoko...
"In other words, right now... you're asking me to abandon Satoko?"
And so I could not hide the anger that rose in me at Keiichi's words. Not anger at Keiichi. Anger at myself—for choosing something so helpless, so heartless, so cold.
If Satoko ever learned the truth, would she be angry? Would she resent me? ...Would she hate me?
"Looking at the bigger picture—I know that sounds like a selfish excuse. And I'm not asking you to tell me my judgment is right."
"...But unless we can find a better way, this is the only choice I can think of."
"That's why, Keiichi—I need you to keep your feelings in check from here on. And beyond that, I'm certain that Shii and Rena will try to rescue Satoko through more forceful means."
"Whatever it takes, I need you to use your strength to stop them. So..."
"—I can't do that."
changeReason: Changes 'No way' to 'I can't do that'; JPN だめだな expresses inability/refusal rather than disbelief.
Changes 'Meep' to 'Mii'; JPN みぃ is Rika's characteristic vocal tic, romanized as 'mii,' not the English word 'meep.'

TextENGNew: But Keiichi dismissed my anguish without a moment's hesitation, as though it were nothing at all.
"I understand what you're trying to say, Rika-chan. ...But what you're describing is ultimately a gamble, isn't it?"
changeReason: Changes 'settling' to 'gamble'; JPN 駆け引き means strategic maneuvering/bargaining/gambit, not settling or compromising.

TextENGNew: "That's not who I am, and it's not what I want to do. So even if the future you've foreseen is real, Rika-chan... I'm sorry, but no."
"Keiichi..."
"I've heard before about how the villagers treat Satoko with contempt. Her parents were supporters of the dam project, right?"
"...But I don't see why that means there's no other way to save her."
"There might be other chances yet—. And besides, that's not the way I want to earn the village's trust."
"...We cannot face the enemy that lies ahead unless the whole village stands united. And if we cannot defeat that enemy, everyone will come to grief. —Even knowing that, you still refuse?"
"Didn't I tell you? I don't believe in that kind of fate!"
"I don't know yet who this enemy is, but anyone who tries to tear apart our happiness—I will never forgive them, and I will never lose!"
"If that's what it takes, I'll go around and persuade every last person in the village!!"
I cast my eyes down and breathed out in weary exasperation. ...I thought he was a fool, but not to this degree.
"...It might have been better if I'd never told you."
"No, I'm glad you did. ...You don't actually want to hand Satoko over to her uncle either, do you, Rika-chan?"
"...Then what means would you propose?"
"Simple. We'll find a way to stop Rina's theft—so Teppei never has a reason to come take Satoko away!"
...That predictable answer brought a small wave of disappointment. Did he have any idea how many times I had already turned that exact thought over in my head before coming here?
I did not want to abandon Satoko either. The mere thought of seeing those hollow, lifeless eyes again... felt like something tearing loose inside me.
But... I truly could find no other way. What method could possibly exist to make Rina Mamiya abandon her plan—without her succeeding, and without her being killed?
changeReason: New interprets 計画を失敗させず (without making the plan fail) as 'without her being killed,' adding specific context about what failure means here (Rina's death at Rena's hands), which changes the explicit framing.

TextENGNew: "...Keiichi. Even granting that we prevent the theft, in order to keep Rina from being killed and yet still prevent her from leaving Okinomiya with the money—Rina herself has to be the one to give up the plan."
"Which means we cannot turn to the police or to anyone connected with the Sonozaki group. —So how exactly do you intend to stop her?"
In answer to my question, Keiichi folded his arms with a smug little sound and grinned.
...Insufferable. If he gives his usual 'I haven't thought of anything yet' answer, I'm going to hit him with this bottle.
"—Have you forgotten, Rika-chan? There's someone in the club who will lend their strength without fail—if it's for Satoko's sake."
"Mii? ...Oh."
changeReason: Changes 'Meep' to 'Mii'; JPN みぃ is Rika's characteristic vocal tic.

TextENGNew: When I understood who he meant... I sighed quietly. I had already considered what that path might look like.
Yes—the person with ties to the Sonozaki group, the person who would choose any means necessary in a crisis involving Satoko. —That was Shion Sonozaki. And yet...
"How would you explain it to Shii? To begin with, yakuza organizations care enormously about their face."
"If they found out that some young associates had been targeting their safe, they simply could not let those people walk away alive. ...In the end, that would only mean Rina gets killed."
"Well, sure—if you explained it to her normally. But what if Rina's plan had never existed in the first place?"
"...?? M-mii... I'm not quite sure what you mean by that."
changeReason: Changes 'Meep' to 'Mii'; consistent with JPN みぃ romanization.

TextENGNew: I truly had no idea what he was saying. What on earth was Keiichi thinking?
But...
"Come on, leave it to me, Rika-chan. I've got an idea."
And Keiichi smiled—fearless, unbothered.

Looking at that face, all the guilt I had felt about abandoning Satoko, all the revulsion at my own helplessness that had weighed on me until moments ago... seemed suddenly, absurdly, like the worries of a fool. It was the strangest feeling.
"So? How about it, Rika-chan?"

changeReason: Changes 'Meep' to 'Mii'; consistent with JPN みぃ romanization.

TextENGNew: And so I found myself nodding, almost against my will.
Well. All right then.
This boy who had declared that fate was nothing but a thin sheet of paper—what sort of miracle was he going to show me...? I found I wanted to see it.
changeReason: Old translation says 'thin sheet of paper' but JPN 金魚すくいの紙 specifically refers to the fragile paper used in the goldfish scooping game, a vivid Japanese cultural metaphor that both translations simplify but the old further distorts as 'flimsy.'
